# INSTRUMENT — a FORM, not a grid. 16 rows (sampler) / 15 (SoundFont), and the row map
# itself depends on the type. Columns 0..3 and 5 are probed on EVERY row, including the
# ones where they do not exist: a module that fails to answer none() there is how a
# cursor lands on a cell nobody draws. sfp=COUNT,INDEX is the SF2's preset list (engine
# knowledge; the Project does not hold it), which is what the PRESET row's range is made of.
EDIT scr=INSTRUMENT row=0 col=0 sfp=0,0 inst=SM:0.4:80:06:FF:0:80:00:off:0:00:0:00:00:00:-1:off:00:00:FF:FF:0 btn=A => ctx=READ_ONLY|........|0|0|255|1|16|255|0|- act=NONE inst=SM:0.4:80:06:FF:0:80:00:off:0:00:0:00:00:00:-1:off:00:00:FF:FF:0

# MODS — no columns: the cursor is (pair, side, row), and `pair*2+side` is the slot.
# Rows 0..6 are probed against every type, including rows the type does not have (an
# LFO has no REL) — the module must answer read_only() there. A+B resets the WHOLE slot.

# EFFECTS — one column of eight rows (the screen draws fifteen: headers and spacers
# between them). TIME is the interesting one: `delaySync` changes both the cell's
# VOCABULARY (a byte, or one of twelve note divisions) and its cursor RANGE, so the same
# button on the same row does two different things either side of it.

# SAMPLE EDITOR — the sparse row map (1, 2, 8, 10, 11, 13, 14, 16, 18, 19). Rows 3..7 are
# the WAVEFORM and 9/12/15/17 are spacers, and both must answer none(): the whole 0..19
# range is swept so that a row which is not a cell is PROVEN not to be one. Rows 3..8 have
# no context either — their A+DPAD drags the selection edge, which no CursorContext can
# express, so the dispatcher owns it (and tools/ptdispatch covers it).
EDIT scr=SAMPLE_EDITOR row=0 col=0 bed=0 env=0:44100:0 se=0:0:0:0:2:1:2:40:08:0:0:00:0:0:0:[] btn=A => ctx=READ_ONLY|........|0|0|255|1|16|255|0|- act=NONE se=0:0:0:0:2:1:2:40:08:0:0:00:0:0:0:[]

# FXH — the 6x5 grid. Its last row is CENTRED (cols 1..4); cols 0 and 5 there are
# empty and unreachable, and every move that could land on them rounds inward.

# SORT — the file browser's listing order. The browser has no cursor context and no
# handleInput (it navigates, it does not edit), so this is what there is to measure:
# sortItems over a list buildItemList has already put in NAME order.
#
# ⚠️ 34 of the 36 files share ONE mtime, and the SIZE of the fixture is load-bearing.
# sortedBy is STABLE, so a tie must fall back on the name order the build left behind.
# A four-file fixture could NOT prove that: every std::sort falls back to insertion sort
# below a threshold (32 on MSVC, 16 on libstdc++) and insertion sort is stable, so it
# passed. Past the threshold introsort partitions and ties scramble. Measured, not
# assumed — the first version of this fixture had four files and a std::sort went green.
#
# '..' is always first; folders always precede files; the sort orders each GROUP.
SORT mode=DATE_DESC => ..,[alpha],[Zed],zzz,s00,s01,s02,s03,s04,s05,s06,s07,s08,s09,s10,s11,s12,s13,s14,s15,s16,s17,s18,s19,s20,s21,s22,s23,s24,s25,s26,s27,s28,s29,s30,s31,s32,s33,aaa
SORT mode=DATE_ASC => ..,[Zed],[alpha],aaa,s00,s01,s02,s03,s04,s05,s06,s07,s08,s09,s10,s11,s12,s13,s14,s15,s16,s17,s18,s19,s20,s21,s22,s23,s24,s25,s26,s27,s28,s29,s30,s31,s32,s33,zzz
SORT mode=NAME_ASC => ..,[alpha],[Zed],aaa,s00,s01,s02,s03,s04,s05,s06,s07,s08,s09,s10,s11,s12,s13,s14,s15,s16,s17,s18,s19,s20,s21,s22,s23,s24,s25,s26,s27,s28,s29,s30,s31,s32,s33,zzz
SORT mode=NAME_DESC => ..,[Zed],[alpha],zzz,s33,s32,s31,s30,s29,s28,s27,s26,s25,s24,s23,s22,s21,s20,s19,s18,s17,s16,s15,s14,s13,s12,s11,s10,s09,s08,s07,s06,s05,s04,s03,s02,s01,s00,aaa
SORT mode=SIZE_ASC => ..,[alpha],[Zed],zzz,s00,s03,s06,s09,s12,s15,s18,s21,s24,s27,s30,s33,s01,s04,s07,s10,s13,s16,s19,s22,s25,s28,s31,s02,s05,s08,s11,s14,s17,s20,s23,s26,s29,s32,aaa
SORT mode=SIZE_DESC => ..,[alpha],[Zed],aaa,s02,s05,s08,s11,s14,s17,s20,s23,s26,s29,s32,s01,s04,s07,s10,s13,s16,s19,s22,s25,s28,s31,s00,s03,s06,s09,s12,s15,s18,s21,s24,s27,s30,s33,zzz

# KBD — the QWERTY keyboard's state machine. Gestures in, (text, textCursor, keyRow,
# keyCol, layout) out. U/D/L/R move the KEY cursor; A types; B deletes; </> move the
# TEXT cursor; 0/1 switch layout.
#
# ⚠️ insertBefore flips A *and* B together (insert-at + backspace, or insert-after +
# forward-delete); clearOnFirstB makes the FIRST B wipe the field. Both are set at OPEN.
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=- => text=- tc=0 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=A => text=Q tc=1 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=A,A => text=QQ tc=2 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=A,A,A => text=QQQ tc=3 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=D,A => text=A tc=1 kr=1 kc=0 lay=0 key=A cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=D,D,A => text=Z tc=1 kr=2 kc=0 lay=0 key=Z cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=D,D,D,A => text=  tc=1 kr=3 kc=0 lay=0 key=  cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=D,D,D,D,A => text=- tc=0 kr=4 kc=0 lay=0 key=  cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=R,A => text=W tc=1 kr=0 kc=1 lay=0 key=W cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=R,R,A => text=E tc=1 kr=0 kc=2 lay=0 key=E cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=L,A => text=P tc=1 kr=0 kc=9 lay=0 key=P cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=U,A => text=- tc=0 kr=4 kc=0 lay=0 key=  cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=U,U,A => text=  tc=1 kr=3 kc=0 lay=0 key=  cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=D,D,D,A => text=  tc=1 kr=3 kc=0 lay=0 key=  cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=B => text=- tc=0 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0

KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=<,<,<,<,<,A => text=Q tc=1 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=>,A => text=Q tc=1 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=<,>,A => text=Q tc=1 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=<,B => text=- tc=0 kr=0 kc=0 lay=0 key=Q cb=0

KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=1,D,A => text=! tc=1 kr=1 kc=0 lay=1 key=! cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=1,D,D,A => text=< tc=1 kr=2 kc=0 lay=1 key=< cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=1,R,R,A,0,A => text=3E tc=2 kr=0 kc=2 lay=0 key=E cb=0
KBD ib=1 cb=0 init=- g=1,R,R,R,R,R,R,R,R,R,0,A => text=P tc=1 kr=0 kc=9 lay=0 key=P cb=0
